4 Quality Information Document for GLOBAL_OMI_TEMPSAL 
© EU Copernicus Marine Service – Public   
CMEMS-SST-OMI-PUM-
GLO_OMI_TEMPSAL 
Issue 3.0 – November 2025 
1) INTRODUCTION 
a) Product Name  
 
GLOBAL_OMI_TEMPSAL_sst_area_averaged_anomalies and 
GLOBAL_OMI_TEMPSAL_sst_trend 
b) Indicator Evaluation Method 
 
These OMIs are based on daily, global climate sea surface temperature (SST) analyses 
generated by the Copernicus Climate Change Service (C3S). The data are available 
from the originators and from the Copernicus Marine Service (product SST-GLO-SST-L4-
REP-OBSERVATIONS-010-024).  
 
Analysis of the data was based on the approach described in Mulet et al. (2018). The 
processing steps applied were: 
1. The daily analyses were averaged to create monthly means.  
2. A climatology was calculated by averaging the monthly means over the 
period 1991 - 2020.  
3. Monthly anomalies were calculated by differencing the monthly means and 
the climatology.  
4. An area averaged time series (provided within the indicator 
GLOBAL_OMI_TEMPSAL_sst_area_averaged_anomalies) was calculated by 
averaging the monthly fields over the globe, with each grid cell weighted 
according to its area. 
5. The time series in each grid cell and the area averaged time series were each 
passed through the X11 seasonal adjustment procedure, which decomposes 
the time series into a residual seasonal component, a trend component and 
errors (e.g. Pezzulli et al., 2005). The trend component is a filtered version of the 
monthly time series. The trend component from the area averaged time series is 
included in indicator GLOBAL_OMI_TEMPSAL_sst_area_averaged_anomalies. 
6. The slope of the trend component for the time series in each grid cell and the 
area averaged time series was calculated using a robust method (Sen 1968). 
The method also calculates the 95% confidence range in the slope.

catalog_entry.summary: This Quality Information Document describes the evaluation method for sea surface temperature anomalies and trends generated by the Copernicus Marine Service, focusing on the GLOBAL_OMI_TEMPSAL_sst_area_averaged_anomalies product.
